text: Hello, there! My name is Ami Hussain. I'm 26 years old and it's been seven years that I'm working in software industry and lately I have started to work in a company called Praza, which is really good company, but I feel like I'm not doing my job pretty well. Also I have started learning a new programming language called Go. One of my friends told me to learn Rust which is a better choice, but at the end I went with Go. So let's see what happens. I will share a couple of more things later.

title: My Presentation
cleaned_text: Hello, there! My name is Ami Hussain. I'm 26 years old and it's been seven years that I'm working in software industry and lately I have started to work in a company called Praza, which is really good company, but I feel like I'm not doing my job pretty well. Also I have started learning a new programming language called Go. One of my friends told me to learn Rust which is a better choice, but at the end I went with Go. So let's see what happens. I will share a couple of more things later.
recommendations: Your confidence is excellent (92.33/100) and it shows—keep using that steady, clear delivery. To make it even more engaging, add intentional vocal variety: pick 3 key words in your presentation and practice saying them with a slightly higher pitch or stronger stress, then record and replay to confirm the emphasis sounds natural.

Your pace (128.98 WPM) is solid for B1, but you can boost clarity by using planned micro-pauses (even though you currently show 0 pauses). Mark your script with a “/” after each main idea and practice a 0.5–1 second pause there; this helps listeners process and makes you sound more in control.

Because you have 0 fillers and 0 hesitations, challenge yourself with a fluency upgrade: practice 2–3 longer sentences that use linking phrases (e.g., “First…”, “In addition…”, “As a result…”) and deliver them in one smooth breath. If you run out of breath, shorten the sentence or add a planned pause—don’t rush.
